facts.statement: Microsoft Corporation is listed on Nasdaq under ticker MSFT, and its SEC SIC description is "Services-Prepackaged Software."
Microsoft Corporation is incorporated in Washington and lists its principal executive office at One Microsoft Way, Redmond, Washington 98052-6399.
Microsoft reported FY2025 revenue of $211.915 billion.
Microsoft reported FY2025 net income of $72.361 billion.
Microsoft reported FY2025 operating cash flow of $87.582 billion.
Microsoft reported FY2025 research and development expense of $27.195 billion.
At FY2025, Microsoft reported stockholders' equity of $206.223 billion, long-term debt of $42.688 billion, and cash and equivalents of $18.315 billion.
Microsoft's 2025 10-K XBRL tagging references the reporting members Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing.

Microsoft's 2025 10-K XBRL tagging references Microsoft 365 Commercial Products and Cloud Services, Microsoft 365 Consumer Products and Cloud Services, Dynamics Products and Cloud Services, LinkedIn Corporation, Server Products and Cloud Services, Windows and Devices, Gaming, and Search and News Advertising.
Microsoft's 2025 10-K XBRL tagging includes a Microsoft Cloud member for FY2025.
Microsoft's 2025 10-K XBRL tagging references Activision Blizzard Inc. and OpenAI Global LLC members.
Microsoft's 2025 10-K XBRL tagging references both a 2021 share repurchase program and a 2024 share repurchase program, including a 2024 share repurchase program date of September 16, 2024.
Microsoft's 2025 10-K XBRL tagging references quarterly dividend members for FY2025, including Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4 dividends.
Satya Nadella signed Microsoft's 2025 Form 10-K as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer and Principal Executive Officer.
Amy E. Hood signed Microsoft's 2025 Form 10-K as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer and Principal Financial Officer.
Alice L. Jolla signed Microsoft's 2025 Form 10-K as Corporate Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer and Principal Accounting Officer.
Microsoft's 2025 proxy states that 11 of its 12 Board nominees are independent, including Sandra Peterson as Lead Independent Director.

Microsoft's 2025 proxy names 12 director nominees: Reid G. Hoffman, Hugh F. Johnston, Teri L. List, Catherine MacGregor, Mark A. L. Mason, Satya Nadella, Sandra E. Peterson, Penny S. Pritzker, John David Rainey, Charles W. Scharf, John W. Stanton, and Emma N. Walmsley.
Microsoft's 2025 proxy identifies standing Board committees including Audit, Compensation, Environmental, Social, and Public Policy, and Governance and Nominating.

Microsoft's proposed 2026 Stock Plan provides that awards are subject to recoupment under the Company's Executive Compensation Recovery Policy, other recoupment policies the Company adopts, and applicable law, to the extent recipients are covered by such policies or law.

Microsoft management concluded that the Company's internal control over financial reporting was effective as of June 30, 2025, and Deloitte & Touche LLP expressed an opinion that Microsoft maintained effective internal control over financial reporting as of that date.
Deloitte & Touche LLP stated in Microsoft's 2025 10-K that it has served as the Company's auditor since 1983.

Microsoft's 2025 10-K states that while the Company has settled a portion of IRS audits, resolution of remaining matters related to intercompany transfer pricing could have a material impact on the Company's financial statements.

Microsoft disclosed that none of its officers or directors adopted, modified, or terminated a Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement or non-Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement during the three months ended June 30, 2025.
